domain: agroforestry (silvopasture: tree-forage-livestock vertical integration with stratified light competition)
specific_mechanism: Silvopasture is a three-tier production stack: canopy trees, mid-story forage, ground livestock. Productivity tradeoffs are stratified by tree density and microclimate: trees reduce evaporative demand (good for forage in heat), reduce windspeed (good for livestock), but compete for light at high density. Optimal stocking is age-stage dependent (young trees: full light; mature: thin to maintain forage light). Total system biomass is maximized when light-niche partitioning is matched to physiological stratification.
llm_application: Train a multi-task LLM with stratified objective: each expert in a MoE router serves a 'tier' analogous to silvopasture strata — tier 1 'canopy' experts handle high-level semantic structure, tier 2 'mid-story' experts produce forage-content (factual paragraphs), tier 3 'ground-livestock' experts emit token-by-token output. Resource competition: tier-1 expert activations cast 'shade' on tier-2/3 by absorbing routing probability; manage with stage-dependent thinning analogous to silvopasture canopy thinning — schedule routing entropy regularization to favor lower tiers as model matures. Basin-stability: the multi-tier stack stays in productive equilibrium under light-budget changes (compute constraints) by phase-shifting routing toward whichever tier has the surplus.
novelty_claim: MoE-in-LLMs survey (2507.11181), Multi-gate MoE / MMoE, similarity-preserving load balancing (2025+ trend), dual-router instance/task systems, ResMoE compression — none specifically frames experts as a vertical silvopasture stratification with stage-dependent routing entropy thinning to mimic canopy thinning. Open question whether 'silvopasture-style stratified expert stack' is substantively new.
